meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Learn how to use structured SaaS pricing to finish projects faster. An intermediate case study using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship client work.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is often 'scope creep' masked as 'feature iteration.' By implementing value-based SaaS pricing, you force a strict, feature-gated roadmap. Use AI-driven scaffolding tools like Antigravity to build the MVP in hours, not weeks, ensuring your project reaches completion before the motivation fades.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a blank code editor, a looming client deadline, and the crushing weight of a feature set that never seems to end. You start with high-velocity enthusiasm, but by week three, the code base is a tangled mess of half-baked ideas and technical debt. The project stalls, the client gets anxious, and your reputation takes a hit.

It doesn't have to be this way. The secret isn't more discipline; it's a structural pivot. By aligning your technical architecture with a rigid, value-based pricing model, you transform your workflow from an endless research project into a high-speed assembly line. Let's break down how to use modern AI tooling to ship finished products every single time.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Price Tier
steps.description: Stop charging hourly. Create a fixed-price tier that limits the scope to a core MVP, preventing endless feature creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a SaaS pricing schema that maps three tiers (Starter, Pro, Enterprise) to specific, limited feature sets for a B2B productivity tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a pricing_config.json based on the value-based tiers defined in the prompt'

steps.title: Scaffold the MVP Architecture
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the foundational boilerplate that enforces your pricing constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Stripe integration, Prisma ORM, and middleware that restricts access based on the pricing tier stored in the database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Initialize the project structure using the scaffolded architecture from Antigravity'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write the logic that checks the user's subscription tier before rendering components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a reusable 'FeatureGate' wrapper component that checks the current user's subscription tier against the required tier for a specific feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file 'components/FeatureGate.tsx' --instruction 'Implement the logic to block access to premium features based on DB status'

steps.title: Automate Subscription Billing
steps.description: Hook up Stripe to your pricing model to ensure revenue is tied directly to the code you ship.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a webhook handler for Stripe that updates the user's 'subscription_tier' in the database upon successful checkout.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create the stripe-webhook.ts file and integrate it with the existing Prisma schema'

steps.title: Final QA and Shipping
steps.description: Run a final audit to ensure no features are accessible without the correct pricing tier.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests using Playwright to verify that unauthorized users cannot access premium routes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Execute the test suite and verify that all pricing restrictions are enforced'

pro_tips: Always build the paywall first; if you can't monetize a feature, you shouldn't be building it.
Use Claude Code to refactor your code into smaller modules every 4 hours to prevent technical debt build-up.
Never ship a feature that isn't mapped to a specific pricing tier in your database.
Automate your deployments via CI/CD pipelines so the act of shipping becomes a single-click event.
Keep your README updated via AI; if you can't explain the project in five sentences, your scope is still too wide.
content.next_steps: After deploying your MVP, monitor your Stripe dashboard for the first conversion and use the feedback to iterate on only the most requested premium features.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical framework to stop stalling and start shipping. Every day you spend in 'development hell' is a day you aren't collecting revenue. Take control of your workflow, enforce your boundaries, and build a sustainable freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
